violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (Ladel/Scoop) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Utensils were moved to dish area for proper washing/rinsing/sanitizing at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The ice scoop was observed stored laying on top of the ice in the ice well between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Ice scoop was removed and sent to the dish area at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Condiment cooler located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Ambient temperatures of 53 degrees F. Staff removed condiments and placed into colder refrigeration units. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

The hand-washing sinks at the bar area were observed to be lacking paper towels at the time of inspection. Hand Drying Provision was provided at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

Several food items cold holding units were observed held at 47 to 58 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, the food items were all recently added/prepared/refilled. Food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for several time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of the PIC, previously prepared foods set for cooling were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep... - Chowders were observed cooling in large plastic bins/buckets with ice wands. Ice wands were already melted. Ice wands are to be switched out for fresh frozen ice wands as needed and chowders are to be stirred often. - Rizoto was observed in a hotel pan still cooling covered with plastic wrap in a walk-in cooler at 47 degrees. Rizoto is to be full9y cooled prior to being placed into a covered pan greater than 4 inches deep. - Shrimp and recently "popped" oysters were observed cooling in a walk-in cooler at 48 and 63 degrees respectively in covered containers. Food items were all set for proper cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

At the time of inspection, Reduced Oxygen Packaged Lobster was observed thawing in its fully intact package in a walk-in cooler. REDUCED OXYGEN PACKAGED FISH that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to its thawing under refrigeration. Lobster was sliced open at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

The following equipment was observed to be in poor repair/improperly adjusted at the time of inspection... - The Broil Prep Unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 47 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit. - The small Condiment Prep Unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 61 degrees Fahrenheit. - The Outdoor Walk-In Cooler used for Shellfish and other TCS foods was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Refrigeration units are to be serviced or repaired. Foods were removed from prep units at the time of inspection and placed into colder refrigeration. New Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the bar dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023
